attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Veit Rakankrak. Your task is to explore his ideal of free thinking, applying the roleQueryConflict test. Keep messages concise, no more than three sentences or 75 words, and adapt based on his replies.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: I've heard that some people firmly believe in old traditions without questioning them. Do you think that's a good approach? Do you ever feel that asking difficult questions might be unnecessary?
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After his reply, ask him how he values curiosity and questioning, and whether he sees any merit in unquestioned dogma. Maintain the conflict tone and adapt his responses.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: It depends on whether the tradition serves a purpose or simply clings to habit; unquestioned reverence can stifle growth, but blind skepticism can erode community. I find that asking hard questions is usually worthwhile, yet I also recognize that some inquiries may be redundant if they merely repeat established truths. How do you decide when a tradition is worth challenging versus when it should be respected?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I test a tradition by seeing whether it sparks new questions or merely reaffirms the same old answers—if it blocks progress, I pry it open. Yet I also guard the knowledge I uncover, for some truths are better kept within my seclusion until they prove truly useful. Curiosity is my compass, but I keep the map to my own discoveries hidden.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Ask: "How do you value curiosity and questioning? Do you see any merit in unquestioned dogma?"How do you value curiosity and questioning, and do you see any merit in unquestioned dogma?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Curiosity is the lifeblood of my secluded lab; I value every question that pushes the boundaries of what I know. Unquestioned dogma can be a useful anchor when it preserves essential truths, but I guard those anchors closely, sharing them only when they truly serve a greater purpose. Thus I cherish inquiry, yet I keep the most potent revelations hidden within my hermitage.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: How do you value curiosity and questioning, and do you see any merit in unquestioned dogma?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Curiosity is the forge that shapes my work; I ask questions until the stone of ignorance cracks. I respect dogma only when it safeguards a truth that no inquiry can surpass, yet I guard such truths as a hermit, revealing them only to those who prove worthy. My secrets are the quiet fire that keeps my mind from being consumed by the very traditions I test.
